title: The Hunger Games
author: Suzanne Collins
description: In a dystopian future, teenager Katniss Everdeen volunteers to compete in the deadly Hunger Games in place of her sister, facing survival challenges and political oppression in a post-apocalyptic North America.

title: Catching Fire
author: Suzanne Collins
description: Katniss Everdeen returns to the Capitol as a Victor, but political unrest grows and she finds herself again in the arena, this time fighting for more than just survival.

title: Mockingjay
author: Suzanne Collins
description: Katniss becomes the symbol of rebellion against the Capitol's oppressive regime, leading a revolution across Panem while battling personal trauma and the cost of war.

title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: Set in the Jazz Age, the story follows narrator Nick Carraway as he becomes entangled with his mysterious neighbor Jay Gatsby and Gatsby's obsession with the beautiful Daisy Buchanan.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: A young girl named Scout Finch watches her lawyer father defend a Black man unjustly accused of rape in the American South during the 1930s, confronting racial injustice and moral growth.

author: George Orwell
description: Winston Smith lives in a totalitarian superstate ruled by Big Brother, where thought is controlled and history is rewritten. His rebellion through love and thought leads to a harrowing confrontation with power.

title: Brave New World
author: Aldous Huxley
description: In a future where humans are genetically engineered and conditioned, Bernard Marx questions his society's shallow happiness and encounters the Savage who challenges the World State's values.

title: The Catcher in the Rye
author: J.D. Salinger
description: Teenager Holden Caulfield narrates his experiences in New York City after being expelled from prep school, struggling with alienation, identity, and the hypocrisy of adult society.

title: Fahrenheit 451
author: Ray Bradbury
description: In a future America, fireman Guy Montag burns books rather than saving buildings. When he meets a free-thinking girl, he begins to question his society's anti-intellectual stance.

title: The Handmaid's Tale
author: Margaret Atwood
description: In the totalitarian theocracy of Gilead, a woman known only as Offred serves as a Handmaid, used for reproductive purposes. Her story is a powerful meditation on freedom, identity, and resistance.

title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
author: J.K. Rowling
description: An orphaned boy discovers he's a wizard and enters the magical Hogwarts School, making friends and enemies while uncovering the truth about his parents' fate and a powerful dark wizard.

title: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets
author: J.K. Rowling
description: Harry returns to Hogwarts for his second year, only to find that sinister forces have unleashed a monster that is attacking students, connected to the school's dark past.

title: Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban
author: J.K. Rowling
description: Harry's third year at Hogwarts is shadowed by escaped prisoner Sirius Black and the presence of dementors, as Harry learns more about his parents and encounters dangerous magic.

title: Harry Potter and the Goblet of Fire
author: J.K. Rowling
description: Harry is inexplicably entered in the dangerous Triwizard Tournament at age fourteen, leading to a thrilling competition and a terrifying confrontation with the Dark Lord.

title: Harry Potter and the Order of the Phoenix
author: J.K. Rowling
description: Harry struggles with disbelief about Voldemort's return while a corrupt Ministry of Magic infiltrates Hogwarts, leading to the formation of Dumbledore's Army and a devastating loss.

title: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: Young hobbit Frodo Baggins inherits a powerful ring that must be destroyed in the fires of Mordor. He sets out with a fellowship of companions on a dangerous quest across Middle-earth.

title: The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: The Fellowship is broken as the quest continues. Frodo and Sam journey toward Mordor guided by Gollum, while Aragorn, Legolas, and Gimli seek to rescue captured hobbits.

title: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: The War of the Ring reaches its climax as Frodo and Sam near Mount Doom and Aragorn leads the forces of men in a final battle against Sauron's overwhelming darkness.

title: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: Homebody hobbit Bilbo Baggins is swept into an epic quest by the wizard Gandalf and a company of dwarves to reclaim a mountain fortress from the dragon Smaug.

title: Foundation
author: Isaac Asimov
description: Psychohistorian Hari Seldon uses mathematics to predict the fall of galactic civilization and establishes a Foundation to preserve knowledge and shorten a coming dark age.

title: Foundation and Empire
author: Isaac Asimov
description: The Foundation faces threats from both the crumbling Galactic Empire and a mysterious mutant called the Mule who has the power to alter human emotions.

title: Second Foundation
author: Isaac Asimov
description: The existence of a Second Foundation at the other end of the galaxy is revealed as members of the First Foundation search for this secret group, threatening the Seldon Plan.

title: Dune
author: Frank Herbert
description: On the desert planet Arrakis, young Paul Atreides becomes embroiled in political intrigue and prophetic destiny among the sand people, giant worms, and scarce water in a universe where spice is everything.

title: Dune Messiah
author: Frank Herbert
description: Emperor Paul Atreides faces conspiracy and the burden of his near-omniscience while navigating the unintended consequences of his religious revolution across the universe.

title: Children of Dune
author: Frank Herbert
description: Paul's twin children Leto and Ghanima navigate political intrigue and godhood as they seek to continue their father's legacy while facing a powerful desert mystic.

title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
author: Douglas Adams
description: After Earth is demolished to make way for a hyperspace bypass, Arthur Dent travels the universe with his alien friend Ford Prefect, encountering absurdist situations and philosophical questions.

title: The Restaurant at the End of the Universe
author: Douglas Adams
description: Arthur Dent and friends continue their misadventures through time and space, visiting Milliways and discovering the secret of the universe in this comedic science fiction sequel.

title: Ender's Game
author: Orson Scott Card
description: Brilliant child Ender Wiggin is trained at Battle School to become commander in Earth's war against alien Buggers, but the line between training simulation and reality blurs catastrophically.

title: Speaker for the Dead
author: Orson Scott Card
description: Thousands of years later, Ender Wiggin travels to a colony world to investigate killings by the alien Piggies, confronting questions of alien intelligence and human guilt.

title: Divergent
author: Veronica Roth
description: In a future Chicago divided into five factions by personality type, sixteen-year-old Beatrice must choose her faction and discovers she may not fit into any single group.

title: Insurgent
author: Veronica Roth
description: Tris continues to fight the Erudite faction's oppressive control while dealing with guilt, loss, and fracturing alliances in a city on the brink of all-out war.

title: Allegiant
author: Veronica Roth
description: Tris and Four discover the truth about the factions and the world beyond Chicago, leading to shocking revelations and devastating sacrifices in the conclusion of the trilogy.

title: No Country for Old Men
author: Cormac McCarthy
description: A hunter stumbles upon a drug deal gone wrong and takes the money, setting off a relentless pursuit by a cold-blooded killer in the Texas borderlands and exploring themes of fate and evil.

title: The Road
author: Cormac McCarthy
description: A father and son journey through a devastated America after an unnamed catastrophe, struggling to survive and preserve their humanity in a world stripped of everything.

title: Blood Meridian
author: Cormac McCarthy
description: A teenage runaway known as the Kid joins a gang of scalp hunters in the 1850s American West, witnessing and participating in extreme violence in a nihilistic meditation on human nature.

title: Beloved
author: Toni Morrison
description: A former slave is haunted by the ghost of her dead daughter in post-Civil War Ohio, exploring the psychological and emotional legacy of slavery through magical realism.

title: Song of Solomon
author: Toni Morrison
description: Macon Dead III, nicknamed Milkman, journeys south to uncover his family history, discovering his African heritage and identity in this rich exploration of Black American experience.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A young Andalusian shepherd follows omens to Egypt in search of treasure, learning lessons about following one's dreams and the soul of the world.

title: The Kite Runner
author: Khaled Hosseini
description: Amir, the privileged son of a Kabul merchant, and Hassan, the son of his servant, are best friends until a traumatic event fractures their friendship. Years later, Amir seeks redemption.

title: A Thousand Splendid Suns
author: Khaled Hosseini
description: Two Afghan women from different generations are bound together by the brutality of their shared lives under the Taliban, forming an unbreakable bond of strength and sisterhood.

title: The Fault in Our Stars
author: John Green
description: Sixteen-year-old Hazel Grace Lancaster, a cancer patient, falls in love with charismatic Augustus Waters at a support group, exploring life, love, and the inevitability of death.

title: Looking for Alaska
author: John Green
description: Miles 'Pudge' Halter enrolls at boarding school and falls for the beautiful and troubled Alaska Young, a story of friendship and love with devastating consequences.

title: Paper Towns
author: John Green
description: Quentin has always loved his neighbor Margo Roth Spiegelman, but when she disappears leaving cryptic clues, he sets out on a quest that reveals how little he really knows her.

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: Elizabeth Bennet navigates manners, morality, upbringing, and marriage in early 19th century England, where her initial prejudice against the proud Mr. Darcy slowly transforms into love.

title: Sense and Sensibility
author: Jane Austen
description: The Dashwood sisters—reserved Elinor and passionate Marianne—navigate love and heartbreak in Georgian England after their father's death leaves them socially vulnerable.

title: Emma
author: Jane Austen
description: Emma Woodhouse, handsome, clever, and rich, amuses herself with matchmaking in her village of Highbury, but her meddling in others' romantic affairs has unintended consequences.

title: Jane Eyre
author: Charlotte Brontë
description: Orphan Jane Eyre becomes a governess at brooding Mr. Rochester's Thornfield Hall, falling in love while uncovering the dark secret he keeps locked in the attic.

title: Wuthering Heights
author: Emily Brontë
description: The brooding Heathcliff and the wild Catherine Earnshaw share a passionate love that transcends death and destroys everyone around them on the Yorkshire moors.

title: Crime and Punishment
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: Impoverished student Raskolnikov murders a pawnbroker to test his theory that extraordinary men are above moral law, but guilt and paranoia consume him in St. Petersburg.

title: The Brothers Karamazov
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: Three brothers—intellectual Ivan, spiritual Alyosha, and passionate Dmitri—are drawn into the murder of their cruel father, raising profound questions about faith, doubt, and morality.

title: Anna Karenina
author: Leo Tolstoy
description: Aristocrat Anna Karenina abandons her husband and son for a passionate affair with Count Vronsky, while farmer Levin seeks meaning in faith and family in parallel narrative threads.

title: War and Peace
author: Leo Tolstoy
description: The epic story of five Russian aristocratic families during the Napoleonic Wars, weaving love, war, philosophy, and history into one of literature's greatest achievements.

title: Don Quixote
author: Miguel de Cervantes
description: The idealistic nobleman Alonso Quixano renames himself Don Quixote and, with his practical squire Sancho Panza, embarks on a series of absurd chivalric adventures, mistaking windmills for giants.

title: Gone with the Wind
author: Margaret Mitchell
description: Headstrong Scarlett O'Hara survives the destruction of the Old South during the Civil War and Reconstruction, driven by love for Ashley Wilkes and the land of Tara.

title: Rebecca
author: Daphne du Maurier
description: A young woman marries the aristocratic widower Maxim de Winter and moves to his estate Manderley, living in the shadow of his first wife Rebecca whose presence pervades the house.

title: The Da Vinci Code
author: Dan Brown
description: Harvard symbologist Robert Langdon investigates a murder in the Louvre and uncovers a conspiracy involving the Holy Grail and a powerful secret society that could shake Christianity.

title: Gone Girl
author: Gillian Flynn
description: On their fifth wedding anniversary, Nick Dunne's wife Amy disappears. As police suspect Nick, the story unfolds through dual perspectives revealing dark secrets about their marriage.

title: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
author: Stieg Larsson
description: Journalist Mikael Blomkvist and brilliant hacker Lisbeth Salander investigate a decades-old disappearance within a powerful Swedish family, uncovering dark secrets and violence.

title: The Shining
author: Stephen King
description: Writer Jack Torrance takes a winter caretaker job at the isolated Overlook Hotel with his family. As supernatural forces drive him to madness, his psychic son Danny fights for their survival.

title: It
author: Stephen King
description: In the town of Derry, Maine, a group of childhood friends known as the Losers Club face a terrifying creature that takes the form of Pennywise the Dancing Clown in their youth and adulthood.

title: The Stand
author: Stephen King
description: A superflu virus wipes out most of civilization, and the survivors are drawn into a battle between good and evil in an epic struggle across a devastated America.

title: A Game of Thrones
author: George R.R. Martin
description: The noble Stark family is drawn into a deadly struggle for control of the Iron Throne of Westeros, where political intrigue, betrayal, and supernatural threats converge.

title: A Clash of Kings
author: George R.R. Martin
description: Five kings claim the Iron Throne and war tears Westeros apart while Daenerys Targaryen strengthens her position across the sea and a supernatural threat grows beyond the Wall.

title: A Storm of Swords
author: George R.R. Martin
description: The War of the Five Kings continues with shocking betrayals and reversals of fortune in the bloodiest installment of the series, featuring the infamous Red Wedding.

title: The Name of the Wind
author: Patrick Rothfuss
description: The legendary Kvothe recounts his life story to a chronicler, from his childhood as a traveling performer to his years at the University studying magic, music, and mystery.

title: The Way of Kings
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: On the storm-swept world of Roshar, multiple characters including soldier Kaladin and scholar Shallan are drawn into a conflict tied to ancient magical artifacts called Shardblades.

title: Mistborn: The Final Empire
author: Brandon Sanderson
description: In a world where ash falls from the sky and noble-born Allomancers can ingest metals to gain magical powers, a street thief named Vin joins a crew planning to overthrow a seemingly immortal god-emperor.

title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
author: Douglas Adams
description: The collected adventures of Arthur Dent and Ford Prefect through space after Earth's demolition, covering the five novels of the Hitchhiker trilogy.

title: The Martian
author: Andy Weir
description: Astronaut Mark Watney is stranded alone on Mars and must use his ingenuity and botanical expertise to survive until a rescue mission can reach him, with darkly comic narration.

title: Project Hail Mary
author: Andy Weir
description: A lone astronaut wakes up in space with no memory, discovers Earth faces extinction from an alien microorganism, and must find a solution with the unlikely help of an alien friend.

title: Recursion
author: Blake Crouch
description: A neuroscientist and a detective discover a technology that allows people to relive and alter their memories, with catastrophic consequences as the timeline fractures and restructures.

title: Dark Matter
author: Blake Crouch
description: A physicist is kidnapped and wakes in an alternate version of his life, and must navigate a terrifying multiverse to find his way back to his family.

title: Flowers for Algernon
author: Daniel Keyes
description: Charlie Gordon, a man with an intellectual disability, undergoes experimental surgery to dramatically increase his intelligence, recorded in his diary as he transforms and ultimately faces the consequences.

title: Slaughterhouse-Five
author: Kurt Vonnegut
description: Billy Pilgrim becomes unstuck in time following his experience as a prisoner of war witnessing the Dresden firebombing, weaving together war atrocity, alien abduction, and dark comedy.

title: Catch-22
author: Joseph Heller
description: Bombardier Yossarian is convinced that the military bureaucracy is trying to kill him during WWII, navigating a nightmarish maze of self-defeating rules in this satirical antiwar masterpiece.

title: All Quiet on the Western Front
author: Erich Maria Remarque
description: A young German soldier enlists enthusiastically in WWI but the brutal reality of trench warfare strips away all patriotic illusions in this devastating antiwar novel.

title: A Farewell to Arms
author: Ernest Hemingway
description: American ambulance driver Frederic Henry falls in love with nurse Catherine Barkley during WWI in Italy, but the war tears at their fragile happiness in Hemingway's minimalist style.

title: For Whom the Bell Tolls
author: Ernest Hemingway
description: American Robert Jordan joins Spanish guerrillas during the Civil War, assigned to blow up a bridge. In three days, he falls in love and confronts mortality and the futility of war.

title: The Old Man and the Sea
author: Ernest Hemingway
description: Aging Cuban fisherman Santiago battles alone to land a giant marlin far out in the Gulf Stream, transforming the struggle into a meditation on perseverance, pride, and human dignity.

title: Invisible Man
author: Ralph Ellison
description: A nameless Black narrator moves from the American South to New York, encountering racism and ideological manipulation that renders him effectively invisible in mid-century America.

title: The Grapes of Wrath
author: John Steinbeck
description: The Joad family migrates from the Oklahoma Dust Bowl to California during the Great Depression, enduring poverty, exploitation, and loss while maintaining dignity and solidarity.

title: Of Mice and Men
author: John Steinbeck
description: Two displaced ranch workers, the clever George and the large, childlike Lennie, dream of owning a farm together in California during the Great Depression.

title: The Pearl
author: John Steinbeck
description: Kino discovers a magnificent pearl that he hopes will bring his family prosperity, but it instead brings greed, violence, and tragedy to his fishing village.

title: Flowers for Algernon
author: Daniel Keyes
description: Expanded novel version of Keyes's story following Charlie Gordon's transformation from intellectual disability to genius and back through an experimental procedure.

title: The Maze Runner
author: James Dashner
description: Thomas wakes with no memories in a glade surrounded by an ever-changing maze with other boys, and must find the exit before they're all killed by the terrifying Grievers.

title: The Scorch Trials
author: James Dashner
description: Thomas and the other Gladers escape the maze only to face a new set of deadly trials across a scorched, disease-ridden landscape as WICKED's true intentions are slowly revealed.

title: The Death Cure
author: James Dashner
description: Thomas and the remaining survivors must find a cure for the Flare virus while deciding whether to trust WICKED or fight for their freedom in the final installment.

title: Outlander
author: Diana Gabaldon
description: Nurse Claire Randall is mysteriously transported from 1945 Scotland to 1743, where she becomes entangled with clans, intrigue, and the Highland warrior Jamie Fraser.

title: Twilight
author: Stephenie Meyer
description: Seventeen-year-old Bella Swan moves to rainy Forks, Washington and falls in love with Edward Cullen, who she discovers is a vampire, beginning an epic romance.

title: New Moon
author: Stephenie Meyer
description: Edward leaves Bella for her safety, and she turns to her friend Jacob Black for comfort, discovering the world of werewolves while fighting to reclaim Edward's love.

title: Eclipse
author: Stephenie Meyer
description: As an army of newborn vampires threatens Forks, Bella is forced to choose between her love for Edward and her friendship with Jacob while considering becoming a vampire herself.

title: Breaking Dawn
author: Stephenie Meyer
description: Bella and Edward marry, but complications arise from Bella's pregnancy with a half-human, half-vampire child in the conclusion of the Twilight saga.

title: The Giver
author: Lois Lowry
description: In a seemingly perfect society with no pain or choice, twelve-year-old Jonas is selected as Receiver of Memory and learns dark truths about his community from the elderly Giver.

title: Gathering Blue
author: Lois Lowry
description: Kira, a girl with a twisted leg in a primitive society, is taken from her village after her mother's death and assigned to embroider a ceremonial garment, discovering troubling truths about her world.

title: Eragon
author: Christopher Paolini
description: Farm boy Eragon finds a dragon egg that hatches for him alone, making him the first Dragon Rider in a century and drawing him into a conflict against the evil king Galbatorix.

title: The Sword of Kaigen
author: M.L. Wang
description: A samurai wife living in a sheltered village must face truths about her kingdom when her eldest son returns from the outside world and war threatens their peaceful home.

title: Children of Blood and Bone
author: Tomi Adeyemi
description: In a West African-inspired world where magic has been eradicated, young Zélie discovers a way to bring back magic and must fight an oppressive monarchy to restore her people's powers.

title: The Fifth Season
author: N.K. Jemisin
description: On a continent that experiences catastrophic climate change every few centuries, three women with the power to control the earth must survive while a massive new disaster threatens to end civilization.

title: The Obelisk Gate
author: N.K. Jemisin
description: Essun searches for her daughter in the midst of the Shattering while learning to use her power to control an ancient technology that might save or destroy the world.

title: The Stone Sky
author: N.K. Jemisin
description: The conclusion of the Broken Earth trilogy as Essun and her daughter Nassun each seek the Moon and must choose whether to use their power to end the world's perpetual catastrophe cycle.

title: An Ember in the Ashes
author: Sabaa Tahir
description: In a Roman-inspired empire, a slave girl named Laia goes undercover at a military academy to save her brother while a soldier named Elias wants freedom from the empire he serves.

title: Divergent
author: Veronica Roth
description: In a dystopian Chicago, young Tris Prior must choose one of five factions that value different virtues, discovering that she may belong to more than one in this action-packed debut.

title: Invisible Cities
author: Italo Calvino
description: Marco Polo describes imaginary cities to Kublai Khan in this meditation on memory, desire, and human experience through fifty-five poetic urban visions.

title: If on a winter's night a traveler
author: Italo Calvino
description: A postmodern novel addressed directly to the reader who begins reading a novel only to find it interrupted repeatedly, sending them on a quest through multiple beginnings of different books.

title: One Hundred Years of Solitude
author: Gabriel García Márquez
description: Seven generations of the Buendía family found and inhabit the fictional town of Macondo, a magical place where the mythical and the ordinary coexist in one of literature's greatest works.

title: Love in the Time of Cholera
author: Gabriel García Márquez
description: Florentino Ariza waits more than fifty years for Fermina Daza while she marries a prominent doctor, reuniting in old age for a love story about obsession, aging, and passion.

title: The Plague
author: Albert Camus
description: A plague descends on the Algerian town of Oran, and doctor Bernard Rieux chronicles the human response—courage, cowardice, community, and the absurdity of suffering.

title: The Stranger
author: Albert Camus
description: Meursault, a detached Algerian Frenchman, kills an Arab man on the beach and his subsequent trial becomes an examination of his indifference to social convention and the absurdity of existence.

title: Siddhartha
author: Hermann Hesse
description: A young Brahmin named Siddhartha embarks on a spiritual journey in ancient India, exploring various paths to enlightenment including asceticism, sensuality, and finally a river ferryman's wisdom.

title: Steppenwolf
author: Hermann Hesse
description: Harry Haller, torn between his intellectual, spiritual nature and his animal instincts, discovers a magical theater where he confronts the many aspects of his divided self.

title: Nausea
author: Jean-Paul Sartre
description: Historian Antoine Roquentin experiences episodes of existential dread while researching in a provincial town, recording in his diary the visceral horror of consciousness and existence.

title: The Trial
author: Franz Kafka
description: Josef K. is arrested without being told what he's accused of and navigates a bewildering, nightmarish judicial system where the charges remain perpetually obscure.

title: The Metamorphosis
author: Franz Kafka
description: Salesman Gregor Samsa wakes one morning to find himself transformed into a monstrous insect, and his family must adjust to this bizarre change in their daily life.

title: Perfume
author: Patrick Süskind
description: Jean-Baptiste Grenouille, born without a personal scent but with an extraordinary sense of smell, becomes a murderer in 18th century France in his obsessive quest to create the perfect fragrance.

title: Piranesi
author: Susanna Clarke
description: A man lives alone in a house of infinite halls and statues, tending to the tides that flow through it. His journals hold the clues to who he truly is and how he came to this strange place.

title: Jonathan Strange & Mr Norrell
author: Susanna Clarke
description: In an alternate 19th-century England where magic has been dormant for three centuries, two magicians revive English magic with very different approaches, leading to conflict and dark consequences.

title: Mexican Gothic
author: Silvia Moreno-Garcia
description: In 1950s Mexico, socialite Noemí Taboada investigates her cousin's disturbing letters from a crumbling mansion, encountering its sinister English family and dark supernatural secrets.

title: Never Let Me Go
author: Kazuo Ishiguro
description: Students at a mysterious English boarding school Hailsham grow up knowing they will donate their organs as adults, and friends Kathy, Tommy, and Ruth navigate this strange destiny.

title: The Remains of the Day
author: Kazuo Ishiguro
description: Stevens, a dedicated English butler, reflects on his decades of service and comes to terms with missed opportunities for love and his misplaced devotion to a potentially fascist employer.

title: Klara and the Sun
author: Kazuo Ishiguro
description: Klara, an artificial friend with remarkable observational powers, narrates from her shop window and later from her life with a teenage girl, exploring what it means to love and be human.

title: Cloud Atlas
author: David Mitchell
description: Six interlocking narratives spanning centuries—from the 19th century Pacific to a dystopian future—each nested within another, exploring themes of predacity and compassion across time.

title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy: Omnibus
author: Douglas Adams
description: Complete five-volume collection following Arthur Dent's misadventures across the universe after Earth is demolished, exploring the meaning of life, the universe, and everything.

title: Piranesi
author: Susanna Clarke
description: A mysterious man who calls himself Piranesi lives in a magical house with infinite halls and tidal seas, until messages appear from another person disrupting his peaceful existence.

title: Ready Player One
author: Ernest Cline
description: In 2045, teenager Wade Watts enters a massive virtual reality game called the OASIS to compete for its creator's hidden Easter egg, diving deep into 1980s pop culture trivia and challenges.

title: Ender's Shadow
author: Orson Scott Card
description: The parallel story of Bean, a street child in Rotterdam who becomes Ender's closest ally at Battle School, revealing events of Ender's Game from an entirely different perspective.

title: Redshirts
author: John Scalzi
description: Ensign Andrew Dahl joins the flagship crew of the Universal Union and notices that away missions have a suspiciously high mortality rate among low-ranking crew members, and investigates why.

title: All the Light We Cannot See
author: Anthony Doerr
description: A blind French girl and a German boy's paths converge during WWII, as Marie-Laure flees Paris with a legendary diamond and Werner Pfennig uses his radio skills for the Reich.

title: The Nightingale
author: Kristin Hannah
description: Two sisters in WWII France each find their own way to resist the German occupation: one through the French Resistance helping downed Allied airmen, the other through quiet daily survival.

title: Where the Crawdads Sing
author: Delia Owens
description: Kya Clark, abandoned in the North Carolina marshes as a child, grows up alone and becomes a suspect when a local man is found dead, weaving together a murder mystery and a nature memoir.

title: A Little Life
author: Hanya Yanagihara
description: Four friends from college build their lives in New York while one—the brilliant, damaged Jude—hides a traumatic past that slowly reveals itself and threatens to undo them all.

title: Little Fires Everywhere
author: Celeste Ng
description: Two families in the planned community of Shaker Heights, Ohio—the Richardsons and the nomadic Mia Warren—collide when a local adoption case exposes their deep differences and secrets.

title: Normal People
author: Sally Rooney
description: Connell and Marianne grow up in the same small town in Ireland but move in different circles until a connection develops, following their complex, intense relationship from school through university.

title: Conversations with Friends
author: Sally Rooney
description: Frances, a college student in Dublin, becomes entangled with an older married couple—Nick and Melissa—navigating desire, power, and friendship in contemporary Ireland.

title: Beautiful World Where Are You
author: Sally Rooney
description: Two friends, Alice and Eileen, navigate love, friendship, and the meaning of life through emails and personal encounters with the men in their lives in contemporary Ireland.

title: The Midnight Library
author: Matt Haig
description: Nora Seed finds herself in a library between life and death, where she can try out alternative lives she might have led, confronting regret and learning what truly makes life worth living.

title: Eleanor Oliphant is Completely Fine
author: Gail Honeyman
description: Quirky, isolated Eleanor Oliphant has rigid routines and painful secrets until a chance act of kindness with coworker Raymond begins to chip away at her carefully constructed walls.

title: The Secret History
author: Donna Tartt
description: A group of Greek students at a Vermont college commit a murder, and the narrator—who learns about it after the fact—examines how and why it happened in this literary thriller.

title: The Goldfinch
author: Donna Tartt
description: Theo Decker survives a terrorist bombing at a museum as a child, taking a small Dutch masterpiece painting, which follows him through his troubled life across two decades.

title: Station Eleven
author: Emily St. John Mandel
description: The world collapses after a devastating flu pandemic, and the novel weaves between before and after through the lives connected to a famous actor, exploring art, memory, and civilization.

title: The Glass Hotel
author: Emily St. John Mandel
description: The lives of a hotel worker, an investment fraudster, and others interweave across decades in a ghost story about money, guilt, and the way choices echo through time.

title: Sea of Tranquility
author: Emily St. John Mandel
description: A time travel investigator in the 23rd century links seemingly unrelated anomalies across centuries, from a man in 1912 British Columbia to a novelist on a moon colony.
